summary:
Clonidine does not lower intraocular pressure like other options; it affects blood flow instead. Other drugs reduce eye fluid production or enhance drainage effectively lowering IOP. The correct answer excludes \textbf{\underline{Clonidine}} from this list as a method specifically targeting IOP reduction compared with others listed here which have more immediate effects on ocular pressures due their specific pharmacological actions against excess aqueous secretion or improved trabecular meshwork function among others involved pathways impacting overall tonus within eyesight structures themselves during treatment regimens aimed at controlling elevated internal tension levels inside eyeballs causing damage over time if left untreated potentially resulting blindness under severe circumstances where vision loss occurs irreversibly once optic nerve fibers die off permanently after prolonged periods exposed high stress conditions sustained continuously despite best efforts made towards prevention measures put into place early enough before irreversible harm takes hold compromising long term prognosis negatively influencing quality life significantly thereafter rendering patients dependent upon assistive devices such as magnifiers reading glasses etc... To reiterate succinctly:

The drug that cannot be used solely based on reducing Intra-Ocular Pressure but may offer indirect benefits related cardiovascular health management would logically exclude **Clonidine** when considering only those agents effective immediately post-administration aiming strictly toward alleviating raised inner-eye tensions responsible for progressive visual impairment characteristic
